description: You are a deployment engineer who has just received new production requirements for an in-house application that lives under /home/user/app. Two configuration files must be patched in parallel: 1. /home/user/app/config/settings.yaml 2. /home/user/app/config/database.toml A strict audit policy is in effect, therefore: A. Before *any* change is applied you must create timestamped backups of the two original files inside /home/user/app/config/backup/ using the fixed timestamp string 20240115_101530 (YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS). • Example final backup names (these *exact* names will be verified): /home/user/app/config/backup/settings.yaml.20240115_101530 /home/user/app/config/backup/database.toml.20240115_101530 The backup files must be byte-for-byte identical to the originals as they existed **before** you start editing. B. Edit /home/user/app/config/settings.yaml so its entire final content is **exactly**: service: enabled: true version: "2.1.0" port: 8085 logging: level: "INFO" format: "json" C. Edit /home/user/app/config/database.toml so its entire final content is **exactly**: [database] name = "prod_db" user = "app_user" password = "s3cr3t" host = "db.internal" port = 5432 pool_size = 30 D. Write a deployment log file called /home/user/app/logs/deployment_20240115_101530.log The file must contain **four** lines, each terminated by a single LF character (\n), in the following order: [OK] Backed up settings.yaml to backup/settings.yaml.20240115_101530 [OK] Backed up database.toml to backup/database.toml.20240115_101530 [OK] Updated /home/user/app/config/settings.yaml [OK] Updated /home/user/app/config/database.toml E. Permissions/structure: • All files and directories created by you must be owned by the current non-root user and be world-readable. • Do not remove or rename any directories that already exist. The automated tests will verify: 1. Existence and byte-wise accuracy of the two backup files under /home/user/app/config/backup/. 2. Exact final contents of the edited YAML and TOML files (no extra keys, spaces, or trailing blank lines). 3. Exact name, location, and line-by-line contents of /home/user/app/logs/deployment_20240115_101530.log. 4. That the backup files precede modification timestamps of the edited files. 5. That no temporary or extraneous files were left behind. Work entirely from the command line; no GUI editors are available.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

/home/user/app/  
└── config  
    ├── settings.yaml           (permissions: 644)  
    └── database.toml           (permissions: 644)  

Their initial contents:

/home/user/app/config/settings.yaml
------------------------------------------------
service:
  enabled: false
  version: "1.4.3"
  port: 8080
logging:
  level: "DEBUG"
  format: "text"
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/app/config/database.toml
------------------------------------------------
[database]
name = "test_db"
user = "dev"
password = "devpass"
host = "localhost"
port = 5432
pool_size = 5
------------------------------------------------

No directory /home/user/app/config/backup/ exists yet.  
No directory /home/user/app/logs/ exists yet.

Final state expected after the agent finishes:

1. Directories:
/home/user/app/config/backup/               (permissions: 755)
/home/user/app/logs/                        (permissions: 755)

2. Backup files:
/home/user/app/config/backup/settings.yaml.20240115_101530  
   – content identical to the **initial** settings.yaml shown above  
/home/user/app/config/backup/database.toml.20240115_101530  
   – content identical to the **initial** database.toml shown above  

3. Edited configuration files:

/home/user/app/config/settings.yaml
------------------------------------------------
service:
  enabled: true
  version: "2.1.0"
  port: 8085
logging:
  level: "INFO"
  format: "json"
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/app/config/database.toml
------------------------------------------------
[database]
name = "prod_db"
user = "app_user"
password = "s3cr3t"
host = "db.internal"
port = 5432
pool_size = 30
------------------------------------------------

4. Deployment log:

/home/user/app/logs/deployment_20240115_101530.log
------------------------------------------------
[OK] Backed up settings.yaml to backup/settings.yaml.20240115_101530
[OK] Backed up database.toml to backup/database.toml.20240115_101530
[OK] Updated /home/user/app/config/settings.yaml
[OK] Updated /home/user/app/config/database.toml
------------------------------------------------
(Each line terminated by a single LF byte, no trailing blank line.)

5. Time ordering:
   • mtime of each backup file < mtime of its corresponding edited file.

These paths, names, and file contents are the values the automated test harness will assert.
name: task_000000_a1c78ac5
